principles: Open-source default grants only general assistant capability.
Role templates define baseline skills, MCP servers, model, runtime, and visible zones.
Production deployments should keep business roles and MCP grants in an external baseline file and point ROLE_SKILL_MCP_BASELINE_PATH to it.
MCP tools are discovered from runtime tools/list; this baseline stores server-level grants only.

notes: defaultSkills are installed/enabled by default for a role; optionalSkills are visible but not automatically installed.
mcpServers are server-level runtime grants. Tool-level grants are reserved for future use and should stay empty in the OSS default.
permissionProfile describes platform permissions, not a business job title.
dataScope is documentation only; real data isolation must be enforced by each MCP service using agent/user context.

runtimePolicy.selection: JiuwenSwarm is the default role runtime. If it is unavailable, provisioning fails closed until an administrator configures a supported runtime; no OpenClaw fallback is applied.

uiPolicy.applyFlow: New users start with the general assistant. Add industry roles in an external baseline for production deployments.

general-assistant.name: 通用助手
general-assistant.description: 默认通用岗位智能体，只开放基础对话、文件、技能和平台能力；不默认授予业务 MCP。
general-assistant.dataScope: No business data access by default. Only the current agent workspace and explicitly configured tools are available.

extensionModel.newIndustry: Use the supported industry buckets or extend the schema in code for new top-level industries.
extensionModel.newRole: Add production roles in an external baseline file instead of committing customer-specific roles to the public repository.
extensionModel.newSkill: Marketplace skills should carry roleTag/provider/origin metadata and be granted through admin workflows.
extensionModel.newMcp: Register MCP servers in the runtime, then grant server ids to roles. Tool descriptions are read from MCP tools/list.
extensionModel.userOverride: Per-user skill/MCP overrides should be auditable and should not bypass the MCP service data policy.
